rag_rules: Validator diagnostics must be resolved by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current AADL scope and match expected types.
AGREE contracts and their associated declarations must reside in appropriate annex scopes (component type or implementation) based on their semantic level.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are declared or visible within the same annex block or accessible via proper AADL scoping rules.
Use only scalar types (int, real, bool) in AGREE constant and equation declarations; avoid package-qualified or undefined type references.
Component port assumptions and guarantees should be scoped to component types unless implementation-specific bindings are required.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and must be placed in valid AADL component type or implementation scopes.
Helper declarations like equations and constants must have consistent types between declaration and assigned expression.
anti_patterns: Do not reference identifiers or types that are not visible in the current AADL scope or defined in the annex; this leads to validation errors.
Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes unless explicitly required by the model structure.
irrelevant_topics: Package visibility and with clause requirements
